commit.message:
fix: use path-based versioning for static assets in HA Ingress

HA Ingress proxy strips query parameters from static file URLs,
so ?v=VERSION cache busting was ineffective. Static assets are
now served via /static/v<VERSION>/<file> route, embedding the
version in the URL path to guarantee fresh fetches on upgrade.

Bump version to 2.12.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: CFL-074 CheckUnderflowOverflow global ops budget (timeout fix)

Add global operation counter (100K budget) and reduce max recursion
depth from 100 to 16 in CheckUnderflowOverflow(). The original code
had O(nOps^depth) complexity — crafted calculator ops with nested
if/else/select could trigger >1000s execution in Begin().

Root cause: DoesStackUnderflowOverflow() calls CheckUnderflowOverflow()
twice (underflow+overflow check), each with depth-100 recursion but
no per-call operation budget. SequenceNeedTempReset() already had this
guard (kMaxOpsProcessed=1M) but CheckUnderflowOverflow() did not.

Fix: Add pOpsProcessed parameter (matches SequenceNeedTempReset pattern)
with 100K budget. Timeout PoC drops from 1011s to 37ms.

Verified: upstream iccDEV tools (iccDEV/Build/Tools/) handle the PoC
without issue (iccDumpProfile exits 0 in <1s).

CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(provider,sidebar): inject cloud API key for cloud-tagged model requests [6ogy] (#27)

* fix(modelfiles): use proper ThemeIcon instance to prevent iconPath crash

Replaces `{ id: 'file-code' } as unknown as vscode.ThemeIcon` with a
real ThemeIcon instance via the same createThemeIcon helper pattern used
in sidebar.ts. VS Code internals call .file[0] on iconPath and crash
when it receives a plain object instead of a ThemeIcon.

Fixes ollama-models-vscode-h9yi

* fix(sidebar): track resolved cloud model name for correct stop call

CloudModelsProvider now stores the resolved tag name (e.g.
kimi-k2-thinking:cloud) alongside the base name in warmedModelResolvedNames
when markModelWarm is called.  handleStopCloudModel uses getWarmedModelName()
to look up the resolved name before calling stopModel, preventing the
"model not found" error caused by passing the bare base name.

Fixes ollama-models-vscode-acrx

* fix(provider,sidebar): inject cloud API key for cloud-tagged model requests [6ogy]

- Add getCloudOllamaClient() to client.ts: reads ollama-cloud-api-key secret
  and sets Authorization: Bearer header for cloud proxy requests
- LocalModelsProvider gains optional context param; startModel() uses cloud
  client when warming a cloud-tagged model
- provideLanguageModelChatResponse() detects cloud-tagged runtimeModelId and
  uses getCloudOllamaClient as the per-request client
- Fix LocalModelsProvider constructor arity in sidebar.test.ts

* fix(sidebar): make LocalModelsProvider context param optional

context is only used when handling cloud-tagged models (already guarded)
and is absent in many unit-test call sites. Making it optional eliminates
TS2554 ("Expected 2-4 arguments, but got 1") in those tests.

* test: pass mockCloudProvider as 3rd arg to handleStopCloudModel

The test defined mockCloudProvider but forgot to pass it, causing
the assertions on its methods to always fail.

* fix(extension): flush final-round text when MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS exhausted [q1wb] (#33)

* fix(extension): flush final-round text when MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS exhausted [q1wb]

When the agentic tool loop exhausts all MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS iterations without
a clean break (model keeps requesting tools every round), the last round's
buffered assistantTextParts were silently discarded and never rendered.

Track lastRoundTextParts outside the loop and flush unconditionally after
the loop exits. The break path clears lastRoundTextParts to avoid a double
flush when the loop terminates naturally.

commit.message:
perf: use bulk rebuild path for first-run indexing

When the fingerprint table is empty (first run on a new vault), use
the rebuild() path (single bulk DELETE + bulk INSERT) instead of
incremental_update() (per-file remove + insert). For 10,365 files
this avoids ~114,000 individual SQL statements.

Also log phase timing (scan_ms, read_parse_ms, write_ms) for
diagnosing future indexing performance.

Previous: 230 seconds for 10K pages (incremental upsert path)
Expected: 2-5 seconds (bulk insert in single transaction)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Backend:
- GET /api/teachers: switch from find() to aggregate() with $lookup
  to include reviewCount and avgRating per teacher
- q param now uses $regex (case-insensitive) for partial name/dept match

Frontend (ReviewsPage):
- Teacher search bar with autocomplete dropdown (partial match, live)
- Faculty cards grid (2-col) with name, dept, ⭐ avg rating, review count
- Clicking a card or dropdown item filters reviews to that teacher
- Click selected card again to deselect / clear filter
- All teachers loaded once; client-side filtering for snappy UX
- Removed inline composer bar (search now anchors the UX)

tests/teachers.test.js:
- Updated field assertion: subjects → reviewCount + avgRating

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Always show peer picker menu for RAG sync push/pull

Previously the Menu with peer names only appeared when peers.count > 1.
With a single connected peer, the code fell through to direct Push/Pull
buttons that auto-selected the first peer without showing which machine.

Now always show the peer picker Menu whenever peers are available, even
with one peer, so the user always sees which machine they're syncing with.

commit.message:
Fix SIMD alignment bug, perf enhancements, and JS cleanup (#1236)

- SimdByteScanner: round data length to 16-byte boundary before
  MemoryMarshal.Cast to avoid alignment faults on unaligned tails
- StaticHTMLFragments: replace string concatenation in dropdown loop
  with StringBuilder
- ProxyRouteHandler: pool the 80 KB copy buffer via ArrayPool
- JsonWriterHelper: add TODO for MemoryStream pooling
- BareMetalWebExtensions: fire-and-forget StaticAssetCache.Build,
  remove duplicate NotificationDefinition registration
- RuntimeEntityRegistry: parallelize five sequential QueryAsync calls
  with Task.WhenAll
- lookup-helper.js: cap setInterval with max-attempt counter
- form-validation.js: use event delegation instead of per-element
  listeners

Closes #1162, closes #1145, closes #1147, closes #1148, closes #1149, closes #1189, closes #1190

feat: K8s deployment, Terraform IaC, and AG-UI authentication (password + JWT/OIDC) (#48)

* feat(docs): add k8s deployment.yaml generation in config builder

* add json marshal for tools

* feat(security): add AG-UI authentication middleware (password + JWT/OIDC)

- New pkg/security/auth package with unified middleware supporting:
  - Password auth (config → env → keyring → auto-generate fallback)
  - JWT/OIDC validation with JWKS auto-discovery from trusted issuers
  - Combined mode: JWT checked first, password fallback
- Refactor server.go to use auth.Middleware, remove inline password logic
- AGUIConfig now embeds auth.Config for clean TOML/YAML deserialization
- Terraform: add random_password resource, AGUI_PASSWORD in external secrets
- K8s genie.toml: add [messenger.agui.auth] with password_protected=true
- Config Builder UI: auth toggle controls for password + JWT/OIDC
- Documentation: auth config reference, resolution order, security best practices
- 26 Ginkgo/Gomega tests with 85.4% coverage

* Update docs/js/config-builder.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update CHANGELOG with K8s deployment and AG-UI auth entries

* refactor(auth): decompose Config into sub-structs, use golang.org/x/oauth2 + go-oidc

- Decompose flat Config into PasswordConfig, JWTConfig, OAuthConfig sub-structs
- TOML sections: [auth.password], [auth.jwt], [auth.oauth]
- Rewrite jwt.go to use coreos/go-oidc for cryptographic JWT verification
- Rewrite oauth.go to use golang.org/x/oauth2 for OAuth2 flow
- Add dedicated oauth_test.go and password_test.go
- Update docs/data/docs.yml with new config structure + OAuth docs
- Update docs/js/config-builder.js UI and serializers
- Update genie.toml, server.go, builder.go references

* refactor(auth): enforce single authentication strategy via Authenticator interface

- Removed the monolithic guard struct and fallback complexity.
- User can only choose one authentication source (OAuth > JWT > Password precedence).
- Removed fallbacks from JWT to Password and from OAuth to Password.
- Extracted authentication logic into PasswordAuth, jwtValidator, and OAuthHandler implementing the new Authenticator interface.

* feat(auth): refactor OAuth to generic OIDC and add API Key support

* feat: add coding task type and refactor auth Authenticate to return Principal

- Add TaskCoding ("coding") for pure code generation, algorithmic
  problem solving, and script writing (benchmarked via HumanEval,
  MBPP, LiveCodeBench).
- Update create_agent tool description and JSON schema to surface
  the new coding task type alongside planning, tool_calling,
  terminal_calling, and efficiency.
- Refactor Authenticator.Authenticate to return *authcontext.Principal
  instead of bool, injecting a demo principal when no auth is configured.
- Update CHANGELOG.md with both changes.

* feat(auth): wire Principal into context, update docs and config-builder for OIDC/API keys

- Authenticator interface now returns *authcontext.Principal (user identity)
- Each strategy (password, apikey, jwt, oidc) populates Principal with
  ID, Name, Role, and AuthenticatedVia metadata
- Middleware injects demo principal when no auth is configured
- handleRun uses Principal for MessageOrigin.Sender instead of hardcoded 'agui-user'
- Updated examples/genie.toml.example with all auth sections
- Updated docs/data/docs.yml with OIDC + API keys reference
- Updated docs/js/config-builder.js UI and TOML output
- Added qa/20260307_auth_features.md with manual test scenarios
- Updated CHANGELOG.md

* fix(auth): harden OIDC cookie security, fix password comment, use dynamic port in K8s output

- Fix generatedPasswordBytes comment (16 bytes = 32 hex chars, not 64)
- Add nil-guard for OIDC provider after init
- Set HttpOnly + Secure on state cookie clearance in HandleCallback
- Remove query param password hint from docs (header-only)
- Strengthen auth precedence docs with defense-in-depth guidance
- Use dynamic agui.port in K8s deployment YAML output
- K8s install modal shows kubectl apply hint
- Always apply authMiddleware (no nil check needed, middleware handles no-op)
- Clear run_shell from hitl always_allowed in K8s example

* fix(security): add alg:none JWT rejection, add Secure to logout cookie

- jwt.go: add peekAlg() helper and reject alg:none/empty JWTs
- oidc.go: add Secure attribute to HandleLogout session cookie

* security(auth): remove query param password support to prevent credential leakage

Passwords via ?password= query params leak into server access logs, browser
history, Referrer headers, and proxy logs. Header-only (X-AGUI-Password) is
the secure pattern. Updated tests to verify query params are now rejected.

* update test
